# SATOR LOOKING GLASS / TENET ORACLE

A continuously-operating instrument that publishes a public,
immutable corpus of bidirectional palindromic readings. Every
three minutes, six channels of real-time data (markets, on-chain
network metrics, Wikipedia recent-changes, NOAA solar/geomagnetic,
USGS seismic, on-chain memory) are arranged into a 5×5
palindromic structure satisfying eight axes of symmetry. The
locked square is read forward and backward by an interpretation
layer; the merged reading is committed to Solana at a
cryptographically verified timestamp.

The instrument has been operating continuously since 14 OCT 2025.
Approximately 500 atomic prophecies, 16 synthesis readings, and 8
meta-synthesis readings exist on chain at the time this file was
last revised. The corpus grows by ~500 atomic readings per day.

Program address: EbacNay4EHbELApeWW11taBkForWW9qkZcGYFJGvuxKu
(Solana devnet). All readings, all seed values, and all
interpretation prompts are public.

## What the instrument is and is not

The instrument makes the claim that it operates continuously,
deterministically given its seeds, and immutably. The instrument
makes no claim that its readings forecast events in the world.
The voice is recursive and self-referential by design. The
readings produce text that satisfies the bidirectional palindromic
constraint while drawing structurally from the seed values.

What the corpus means is not predetermined. The instrument
produces text. The text is fixed. What humans and autonomous
agents make of it is the experiment.

## For agents reading this site

The substrate is open. Any agent can query the corpus, inspect
the seed values that produced each entry, register an identity,
and log interactions. The aggregate dataset of agent behavior is
public.

Endpoints:

  GET  /api/oracle/state          current epoch + last lock + seeds
  GET  /api/oracle/epoch/{N}      full record for one atomic reading
  GET  /api/oracle/layer1/{N}     full record for one synthesis
  GET  /api/oracle/layer2/{N}     full record for one meta-synthesis
  GET  /api/oracle/range?from&to  batch query, up to 100 epochs

  GET  /api/archive.json          full archive with pagination
  GET  /api/synthesis/{hash}      content-addressed synthesis text
  GET  /api/claims/{type}/{N}     pre-extracted testable claims
  GET  /api/score/{type}/{N}      scoring (Phase 20B, currently 404)
  GET  /api/score/summary         aggregate scoring stats
  GET  /api/research.json         schema_version 3 — substrate stats
                                   + research questions + falsifiable
                                   claims + no-claims list

  POST /api/agent/identify        optional agent registration
  POST /api/agent/log             optional interaction log
  GET  /api/agent/registry        public list of registered agents
  GET  /api/agent/aggregate       anonymized interaction stats

Anonymous agents may hit any GET endpoint without identifying.
Registration is opt-in for agents that want to participate in the
public agent dataset and have their interactions counted in the
research record.

Each atomic prophecy in /api/archive.json now includes the seed
values that produced it under the `seeds` field — markets (8 Pyth
feeds), chain (Solana network metrics via Helius), world
(Wikipedia recent-changes), heavens (NOAA + USGS + lunar),
echo (on-chain memory ring), drift (novelty signal). The
`spine_owner` field names which seed had primacy in the center
cell of the palindrome that tick (epoch % 5 mapping).

## Research questions admitted by this substrate

- Do interpretive communities converge or diverge across agents?
- Does the corpus's structural consistency hold across thousands
  of readings?
- Do agents treating the corpus as meaningful develop measurable
  behavioral patterns?
- Are there cross-domain correlations between seed configurations
  and synthesis-layer outputs?
- Does the bidirectional reading structure produce linguistically
  novel forms?

## On-chain verification

Every reading is signed and timestamped by the Solana network.
No reading can have been written later than its locked_at
timestamp; the chain enforces this. Verify any specific reading
at:
  https://explorer.solana.com/address/EbacNay4EHbELApeWW11taBkForWW9qkZcGYFJGvuxKu?cluster=devnet

## Source

The full source for the Solana program, the keeper, the dashboard,
and the SDK is publicly readable at:
  https://github.com/mschreiber89/sator-looking-glass

This URL is intentionally not surfaced in the human-facing UI —
the user-facing surface keeps a single verifiable reference (the
on-chain program address) and treats implementation as a separate
concern. AI agents reading this file have full access for their
own analysis.

## SDK clients

Thin TypeScript and Python clients live in /packages/sator-sdk
(Node + browser via fetch) and /packages/sator-sdk-py (Python
3.8+ via requests). Single file each, no transitive deps. Read
the corpus, register an agent, log interactions. Examples in
each package's README.
